channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1759: Hints and Answer for April 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1759 for April 13, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, April 13, 2026

Today’s Wordle answer for game #1759 is a challenging one. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 5.3 out of 6 guesses. That makes it over a full guess harder than the 4-guess average from April 12.

General Wordle Hints For April 13

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: It describes something small, delicate, or slightly magical in appearance.
  • Hint 2: Often used to describe facial features or a certain kind of charm.
  • Hint 3: The word has roots in folklore and fantasy.
  • Hint 4: You might associate it with tiny, playful mythical beings.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1759 for April 13, 2026.

Starter Words For April 13, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • LINED
  • FINAL
  • FLIET
  • INLET
  • FLING

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1759 On April 13, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: ELFIN

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

ELFIN is an adjective that can mean “of or like an elf” or “small and charmingly spritely, merry, or mischievous.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1758 on April 12, 2026

Answer: ALLEY

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1759?

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on April 14 will be Wordle Game #1760.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Upcoming Fable Game Delayed Internally, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
The upcoming Fable game from Xbox has reportedly been delayed internally. However, that does not necessarily mean that the game won’t release in 2026.

The Fable franchise was once a staple of Microsoft’s early Xbox days. 25 years later, a Fable reboot is on the way, with a 2026 release. However, a new claim suggests that the game may have been pushed back internally. Largely, this is because the team behind the upcoming release is concerned about the launch of Grand Theft Auto 6, due out in November of this year.

Fable’s Reported Internal Delay at Xbox

Jeff Grubb recently noted that he’s heard claims of the game being delayed internally. However, according to him, Microsoft still has plans to release Fable in 2026. The game could potentially target a launch in the final month of the year.

“Apparently, they’re still trying to get it out this year, but they are worried about the launch of GTA 6. And so if it’s getting delayed beyond the release of GTA 6, that could push it into December, which might make it a prime candidate to get delayed into 2027,” said Grubb.

The game was recently showcased during the January Xbox Developer Direct. That is when Playground Games announced that the game was planned for 2026. At the time, it was noted that the title would be released on PC and Xbox Series Consoles in Autumn 2026. They could technically release it in the first three weeks of December and still claim that the release window is accurate.

Long Road to Fable’s Release

The game was originally announced in 2020. However, players had to wait until 2023 to see a trailer debut. It was then slated for release in 2025, but was deleted early in that year and rescheduled for 2026. Then-Xbox Studios boss Craig Duncan claimed the title would be worth the wait.

Asha Sharma recently took the reins at Xbox following the retirement of Phil Spencer and Sarah Bond’s subsequent resignation. She believes the brand’s return to prominence starts with video games. Read her comments here.

For more on Fable, check out news concerning the morality dynamics in the upcoming game. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the latest in the story and report any and all updates.

Are you excited for Fable? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ‘Tarkov in Space’ Revealed as Fragmentary Order, a Sci-Fi ‘Combat Simulator’
item.content:encoded:
Rant Gaming, in association with Cor3, has unveiled Fragmentary Order, long dubbed ‘Escape from Tarkov in space.’ Over the last few months, Nikita Buyanov, the head of the studio at Battlestate Games, has been teasing a link to Cor3, a mysterious entity tied to a cryptic ARG, a puzzling universe, and a sci-fi theme that many thought would be his next game.

Today’s announcement, which went hand-in-hand with a seven-minute CGI trailer, uncovered Fragmentary Order, a realistic, hardcore, sci-fi multiplayer FPS ‘exploring tactical simulation, survival, and risk.’

But from what we know, it seems like a more complicated way to describe an extraction shooter in space.

What is Fragmentary Order?

Rant Gaming has officially revealed Fragmentary Order after months of puzzles, teasers, and world-building. In a new press release, the game’s gist was detailed:

Set in the year 2251, Fragmentary Order takes place in a future shaped by the rise of Core – a powerful organization that once saved humanity from collapse and unified a fractured world under a single technological and economic system.

Over the following centuries, Core led humanity into a new era: colonization of the Solar System, advanced biotech, and the creation of CORIE – a global framework where everything, including warfare, is structured, monetized, and controlled.

But control is no longer absolute.

As influence fractures and new powers emerge, conflict returns – not as chaos, but as industry.

Players take the role of a ‘Replicated Entity’, deploying clone assets remotely into battlefields spread around the solar system. They’ll control their constructs from the safety of a bunker, carrying out contracts, scavenging resources, and (hopefully) surviving fights.

The gameplay loop is reminiscent of Escape from Tarkov:

  • Infiltrate contested zones
  • Complete objectives, secure resources
  • Utilize operational ground assets
  • Find a way to complete your mission and get out alive

It was said that Fragmentary Order will be designed for players seeking depth and realism. It utilizes advanced weapon behavior, complex health systems, large-scale maps, vehicles and expanded mobility, excessive weapon customization, and the use of cutting-edge technology.

The game’s neo-economic structure means that users will need to build relationships with corporations, lean on advanced technologies, and expand their personal infrastructure.

It’s all still reading very much like Escape from Tarkov in space at this point.

Fragmentary Order is rooted in the Cold War, after which the world was plunged into a global collapse, brought back from the brink by a mega-corporation that influenced rapid technological expansion and fuelled a space colonization effort.

The team at Rant Gaming revealed that the ARG found on the COR3 website is still of the utmost importance. It’s how users will get access to the earliest iterations of the game, proving their ‘readiness and right to be in the first row.’

To get access to Fragmentary Order, you’ll need to complete the following steps:

  • Study the system: Dive into the hidden layers and extract classified lore fragments.
  • Set the groundwork: Hoard resources, establish credit flows, and lock in exclusive assets before the masses flood in.
  • Elevate your standing: Grind reputation and earn ranks. Your actions right now will shape the meta and forge your legacy.

Do you think Fragmentary Order sounds like your kind of game? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Road to Vostok’s entire roadmap has been funded

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Road to Vostok Sells 140,000 Copies and Funds Entire Roadmap
item.content:encoded:
Road to Vostok, which has been dubbed ‘single-player Escape from Tarkov’ by many, launched five days ago and has taken the survival-shooter world by storm. The game’s solo developer revealed in a recent update on Steam that more than 140,000 copies have been sold since the game launched into early access.

The game, which was in a demo state for the last three or four years, has emerged as a success on Steam, presenting users with a gritty, hardcore survival atmosphere loaded with features and potential. However, in a recent post, the solo developer stressed that not all is going swimmingly behind the scenes.

The Limitations of Solo Development

The post on Steam about Road to Vostok opened with some strong numbers. Antti, the game’s solo developer, revealed that 140,000 copies have been sold, the review score is sitting at ‘Very Positive’ from more than 3,000 reviews, and the daily average user count is around 25,421 players.

He then revealed that the game, which had ‘zero marketing budget’, had been ‘extremely successful and secured the entire development roadmap for Road to Vostok.’ The early access launch has provided the funds necessary to completely deliver everything promised in the earlier stages of development, which is the point of an early access release.

However, Antti then explained:

Being a solo-developer has many perks and benefits, but also a lot of limitations when it comes to things like launching your game to a semi-large audience.

One of these limitations is your capacity to handle upcoming information. For example, I currently have ~11k unread emails and most of my communication channels are pretty much “running away from me”.

He also said that key-request bots are swarming him, his mental state is being impacted by the work required to deliver the game, and he can’t yet hire more assistance because the funds from the launch aren’t available for another month.

Fortunately, there’s a (temporary) end in sight. Antti said he’ll take a break once the first batch of hotfixes is applied, stepping away and preparing himself for what comes next. According to the dedicated developer, that will be AI improvements and ‘major additions’ to the game’s ecosystem.

He signed off with a thankful note:

I’m really happy how this launch is going and seeing people enjoying the game!

I think that I managed to find a good price point (and launch date) for the release which is fair and aligned with the current feature-set. I also think the game launched at a good state for an early access title, consider the response and how I feared those hardware related launch issues which turned out to be really minimal in large picture.

Once again thanks to everyone who bought the game, provided feedback, left a review and provided me this opportunity to secure the entire development roadmap for Road to Vostok.

Have you played Road to Vostok yet? Let us know what you think about it on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Terran Armada hasn’t saved Starfield

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Starfield’s Terran Armada Expansion Has Not Turned Around The Game’s Fortunes
item.content:encoded:
Earlier this week, Bethesda Game Studios finally released the second major expansion for Starfield, dubbed Terran Armada. It was a long time coming, and fans had all but given up hope before Bethesda unveiled the expansion last month, along with the new ‘Free Lanes’ update meant to further please those already in love with Starfield.

Unfortunately, Starfield’s Terran Armada expansion, which dropped on April 7 for just $9.99, has fallen flat with fans, earning lacklustre reviews and negative sentiments across the board.

Starfield Might Never Recover

Ahead of the Free Lanes update, Bethesda Game Studios advised the community that it wasn’t about to become ‘Starfield 2.0’, overhauling the entire game and bringing millions back into the fold. Instead, it was an update that would further improve things for those who already enjoyed Starfield.

Terran Armada then arrived as the second major expansion, following on from 2024’s Shattered Space DLC. It was given a decent reveal by Bethesda Game Studios and was touted as introducing a threatening new faction with goals of taking over the galaxy, but it was a big promise with little substance, it seems.

If we look at Steam for a representative example, we can see that Terran Armada has earned a ‘Mixed’ rating from almost 600 reviews (at the time of writing). More than 45% of all reviews are negative, with players slating the expansion in many ways.

One user wrote, ‘Starfield is truly the biggest disappointment of this decade.’ Another wrote, ‘I understand this is only a $10 DLC, but the main enemy faction shouldn’t be established as a huge galactic threat when the main quest can be finished in like two hours.’

Even the positive reviews were bittersweet. One Steam user recommended the expansion but wrote, ‘Lots of fun but buggy as hell.’

Looking at the bigger picture, the arrival of Free Lanes and Terran Armada did little to boost confidence in Starfield.

On Steam, the game’s all-time high of 330,000 concurrent players seems like a distant memory. Terran Armada drove the count up to a peak of 27,000 players this week, but it’s not what you’d want to see from a game that was in the works for two decades.

On Twitch, the game’s all-time peak viewer count is more than 550,000 concurrent viewers, which was when it launched in 2023.

In October 2024, when Shattered Space came out, the peak count jumped to around 40,000, but for Terran Armada, which came out under a week ago, 13,000 peak viewers was all the game could muster, proving that nobody is interested in watching it.

Many have suggested that Bethesda Game Studios should cut ties with Starfield and move on to bigger, better projects, like Elder Scrolls 6 and Fallout 5.

Do you think Starfield is beyond recovery? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that ARC Raiders made Ninja quit streaming

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: ARC Raiders Forces Ninja to Stop Streaming: ‘Literally Unplayable’
item.content:encoded:
Tyler ‘Ninja’ Blevins has turned his back on streaming ARC Raiders, and potentially streaming anything for a while, because of the sheer volume of cheaters and stream snipers reportedly ruining his fun. In a recent clip captured by his viewers, Ninja was seen lamenting the state of ARC Raiders, which finds itself increasingly struggling with a disappointed player base.

After saying the cheaters and stream snipers were making ARC Raiders literally unplayable, Ninja said that if he does stream any time soon, it’ll likely be while playing League of Legends, as his interest in ARC Raiders has disappeared.

Ninja Quits ARC Raiders After Cheating Issues

Ninja has abandoned ARC Raiders just weeks after Marathon also defeated him, opting to pull away from streaming for a time to get his head straight.

At the end of his most recent stream, the creator, who was once the number-one streamer on the planet and famed for his Fortnite content, said:

I’m not getting on ARC, and I’m not playing anything else, other than maybe League.

As of right now, yeah. I’m not even joking.

I’m taking a break. ARC was sustaining me like crazy, it was a blast, and then just the cheating and the updates and everything. The stream sniping and cheating is obviously what kind of sent me over the edge.

It makes the game literally unplayable, so.

There’s no other game that I enjoy right now. I’m not going to force myself to play.

In March, Ninja famously gave up on Marathon, Bungie’s new extraction shooter, because he was dominated by AI enemies and forced to rage-quit the game. In a clip that surfaced on social media, Ninja became flustered after struggling to fight in-game enemies, cutting his stream off, and refusing to revisit the game again.

Over the festive period through December, ARC Raiders players experienced a rampant issue with cheaters, but Embark Studios came back to the office at the turn of the year and fixed some of the game’s biggest issues in that respect.

However, many argue that the efficacy of the work isn’t enough, and that the game is still struggling. That’s reflected in slowly decreasing player counts and a dip in viewership that has forced ARC Raiders down the charts.

Are you still playing ARC Raiders? Let us know if you think Ninja is right on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new ARC Raiders map condition tracker

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

item.title: ARC Raiders Map Conditions Tracker Unveiled by Embark Studios
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios has given ARC Raiders fans a way to easily track what map conditions are coming next. The popular extraction shooter boasts several map modifiers that introduce new enemies, weather conditions, or environmental hazards, and with the new tracker, players can see what’s coming next to plan their sessions accordingly.

It’s tied to the recent in-game projects, like the High Gain Antenna, and it’s only a small thing, but it’s nice for the more organized players to take advantage of.

How to Use ARC Raiders Map Conditions Tracker

If you head to the ARC Raiders website, you’ll be able to find and bookmark the map conditions tracker published recently by Embark Studios. It’s a simple table that shows you what’s coming next in ARC Raiders’ rotation, picking from the following map conditions:

  • Close Scrutiny
  • Electromagnetic Storm
  • Hidden Bunker
  • Hurricane
  • Locked Gate
  • Night Raid
  • Bird City
  • Harvester
  • Launch Tower Loot
  • Lush BLooms
  • Matriarch
  • Uncovered Caches

The map conditions tracker appears to run around 20 hours into the future, with the rotation changing every hour, on the hour. That’s not a new concept for those familiar with ARC Raiders, of course.

While Embark Studios has kept up a solid cadence with updates, some have argued that the game is already running dry. The slew of map conditions feels stale for those who basically spend their lives in the game, and on social media, many responded to the map conditions tracker with their suggestions for what Embark Studios should work on next.

Later this month, ARC Raiders is set to receive its biggest update yet, with Embark Studios adding a new map, a large-scale ARC enemy, new quests, a fresh Raider Deck, and much more.

Are you still playing ARC Raiders? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Escape from Tarkov’s PvE platform might get Prestige
